title: Alnylam — Relationships
html:
Module 2: The Alnylam-ZoomRx Relationship

Governing Thought

ZoomRx is Alnylam's most comprehensive primary market research partner -- the only vendor embedded across all three therapeutic areas (ATTR, AHP, PH1) with a full-stack study portfolio (PET, ATU, PCA, international multi-country PET, and integrated analysis). The relationship's depth is evidenced by weekly check-in meetings spanning 22+ sessions through April 2026, a project history from #01 (Patisiran Launch Tracker) to #31 (AMVUTTRA PET 2026), and expanding scope into international and cross-study integration work. What makes this relationship structurally defensible is ZoomRx's ability to connect insights across brands and geographies in a way that siloed vendors cannot -- particularly the ADOPT funnel framework that integrates PET, ATU, and PCA into a single adoption storyline.

Partnership Timeline

Period Milestone
Pre-2020 Relationship established with Alnylam; early engagements around ONPATTRO (patisiran) launch tracking (#01 Alnylam Patisiran Launch Tracker)
2020-2022 AMVUTTRA (vutrisiran) launch tracking begins; GIVLAARI PET and Global ATU established; OXLUMO studies initiated
2023 International PET launched across 5 countries (France, Italy, Germany, Spain, Japan) for ATTR. OXLUMO PET baseline wave (Q4 2023) -- first competitive measurement period
2024 International PET Wave 2 delivered. OXLUMO ATU Wave 1 launched. GIVLAARI Global ATU Wave 4. AMVUTTRA TV Tracking. ATTRUBY added to US PET tracking post-approval (Nov 2024). Projects numbered through mid-20s
2025 AMVUTTRA ATTR-CM approval (March 2025) triggers massive research expansion. AMVUTTRA ATU adds cardiologist panels. PCA tracks combination therapy patterns (silencer + stabilizer). AMVUTTRA 2025 Strategy study. OXLUMO PET Final Report (Q3 2025). Patient Message Recall study
2026 AMVUTTRA PET 2026 (#31), PCA 2026, ATU continuation. International PET ongoing. Wainua fielding paused pending CARDIO-TTRansform data. New target list implementation (April 2026). Weekly check-in meetings continue through April 2026. Integrated Analysis framework mature

Key statistic: 31+ numbered projects spanning all three TAs, plus un-numbered studies (Integrated Analysis, strategy work, check-in meetings). The partnership has expanded from a single-product launch tracker to a multi-brand, multi-country, multi-study-type relationship.

Why Alnylam Chose ZoomRx (and Keeps Choosing)

Pillar 1: Full-Stack Research Coverage

What it means: ZoomRx is the only vendor running PET, ATU, PCA, international PET, and integrated analysis across all of Alnylam's brands. No competitor offers this breadth.

Why it matters at Alnylam: Alnylam's brands share the same RNAi platform, the same GalNAc delivery technology, and often the same field representatives. The GIVLAARI + OXLUMO combined PET readout leverages a shared field force. The ADOPT funnel framework integrates PET message tracking with ATU awareness data and PCA prescribing behavior into a single adoption model. This cross-study intelligence is ZoomRx's structural moat.

Evidence: The Q1 2025 Integration of Market Research deck maps each of Alnylam's four TTR strategic imperatives to specific ZoomRx study deliverables -- demonstrating that ZoomRx's studies are not standalone trackers but are explicitly wired into Alnylam's strategic framework.

Pillar 2: International Reach

What it means: ZoomRx runs promotional effectiveness tracking across 5 international markets (France, Italy, Germany, Spain, Japan) in addition to the comprehensive US program.

Why it matters at Alnylam: AMVUTTRA's international expansion is a major growth driver. Each country has distinct competitive dynamics (Spain is Alnylam-dominant; Japan is Pfizer-dominant; France is neuro-centric). ZoomRx provides the only consistent cross-country view of promotional effectiveness, enabling Alnylam to identify best practices (the Spain playbook) and trouble spots (Japan's reach gap) across markets.

Evidence: The international PET synthesis reveals the neurologist-vs-cardiologist divide as a universal pattern across all 5 countries -- a finding that no single-country study could surface. This cross-market intelligence directly informs Alnylam's Strategic Imperative #4 (Establish leadership in cardiology/ATTR).

Pillar 3: Rare Disease Specialization

What it means: ZoomRx has built specialized expertise in ultra-rare disease market research -- managing small sample sizes, benchmarking against rare disease industry averages, and understanding the unique commercial dynamics of patient-finding markets.

Why it matters at Alnylam: Two of Alnylam's three TAs (AHP and PH1) are ultra-rare with fundamentally different commercial challenges than mainstream pharma. In AHP, there are no competitors -- the "competitor" is non-diagnosis. In PH1, sample sizes are tiny (n=29-39 for PET interactions). Standard pharma research frameworks do not apply. ZoomRx's rare disease benchmarks and HCP segmentation models (Enthusiasts/Trialists/Converters/Inexperienced in AHP; specialty-stratified analysis in PH1) demonstrate fit-for-purpose methodology.

Evidence: All GIVLAARI messages are rated above rare disease industry average. OXLUMO PET analysis includes rare disease-specific benchmarks. The HCP adoption segmentation for GIVLAARI was custom-developed for the AHP market's unique dynamics.

Proven Impact: Key Insights Delivered

1. The Neurologist-Cardiologist Divide (ATTR International PET)

Insight: Across all five international markets, AMVUTTRA's SOV among cardiologists trails its SOV among neurologists by 12-34 percentage points. Yet when AMVUTTRA reps DO reach cardiologists, they drive stronger product perception improvement than competitors (91% in Japan, 71% in Spain). Impact: This "perception paradox" (the problem is reach, not receptivity) directly informed Alnylam's Strategic Imperative #4 and provided evidence that field force expansion into cardiology would pay off -- the data showed cardiologists are persuadable, Alnylam just was not reaching them.

2. The Spain SOV Turnaround (ATTR International PET)

Insight: In Spain, AMVUTTRA's SOV flipped from 35% to 56% between Q4 2023 and Q4 2024 -- the most dramatic competitive turnaround in the international dataset. Key drivers: aggressive reach expansion (66% to 91%), strong CM efficacy message delivery, and 96% academic setting. Impact: The Spain playbook became a reference template for other trailing markets. ZoomRx's data showed exactly which levers (reach expansion + CM efficacy messaging + academic targeting) produced the turnaround.

3. The Safety Trap in ATTR Messaging (ATTR PET, All Markets)

Insight: Across all international markets, AMVUTTRA's safety message was the most recalled but the least effective. Meanwhile, the rapid TTR knockdown message (88% reduction in <3 weeks) was the most motivating but recalled in only 33-50% of details. Impact: Identified a consistent rep behavior pattern: over-indexing on safety and under-indexing on the most persuasive efficacy message. This cross-market message optimization finding applies to every country simultaneously.

4. Combination Therapy Emergence (ATTR PCA)

Insight: PCA data showed real-world combination therapy patterns emerging: AMVUTTRA + VYNDAMAX (24 CM charts), AMVUTTRA + ATTRUBY (16 CM charts), WAINUA + VYNDAMAX (6 charts). Impact: First real-world evidence that silencer + stabilizer combinations are becoming a treatment paradigm. This validates Alnylam's Strategic Imperative 1.2 (establish AMVUTTRA as foundational therapy across treatment paradigm) and informed the combination therapy thesis.

5. AHP Disease Perception Shift (GIVLAARI Global ATU)

Insight: US HCPs' primary GIVLAARI treatment goal shifted from "prevent attacks" to "improve chronic symptoms" (59% as top driver). The majority now view AHP as a chronic condition, not episodic. Impact: This reframing supports GIVLAARI's expansion beyond recurrent attackers (Patient Type 1) into chronic symptom patients (Types 4 and 5) -- potentially doubling the addressable market without a label change.

6. OXLUMO Access Perception Gap (PH1 PET + ATU)

Insight: HCPs perceive Rivfloza as more affordable than OXLUMO even among physicians who have never prescribed Rivfloza. This perception gap is consistent across 2 years and 4 studies. OXLUMO's own access story (94% at $0 OOP, 95% payer coverage) is real but underrepresented in promotions. Impact: Identified the single most actionable commercial insight in the PH1 program: Novo Nordisk is winning the access narrative with promotional materials while Alnylam's objectively strong access story is not cutting through.

Growth Trajectory & BD Pipeline

The relationship is expanding along three vectors:

ATTR-CM Launch Expansion

The March 2025 ATTR-CM approval transformed AMVUTTRA from a rare disease neurology product to a cardiology blockbuster. This triggers:

  • Cardiology-specific PET panels (cardiologists added to ATU with 90/wave)
  • PCA expansion to capture combination therapy patterns
  • Strategy studies (AMVUTTRA 2025 Strategy)
  • Potential new study types (MSL effectiveness, patient experience, digital tracking for cardiology)

International Deepening

The 5-country international PET is a platform that can expand in multiple ways:

  • More frequent waves (currently semi-annual)
  • Additional countries (UK, Brazil, and other markets with ATTR presence)
  • ATTRUBY/BEYONTTRA international launch tracking (Bayer in EU, Alexion in Japan, 2026-2027)
  • Multi-country PCA or ATU (currently US-only)

Cross-Study Integration

The Integrated Analysis (PET+ATU+PCA into the ADOPT funnel) represents ZoomRx's highest-value deliverable at Alnylam. This can expand to:

  • Incorporate international PET data into the adoption funnel
  • Add combination therapy adoption as a new dimension
  • Develop cardiologist-specific adoption pathways distinct from neurologist pathways
  • Create real-time competitive dashboards as the market becomes more complex (4 manufacturers)

Rules of Engagement

Based on the study documentation and client interaction patterns:

  1. Lead with cross-study integration. Alnylam has explicitly commissioned integrated analysis (PET+ATU+PCA into ADOPT). Every deliverable should connect to this framework, not stand alone. Single-study readouts are table stakes; cross-study insights are what demonstrate strategic value.

  2. Respect the TA-specific commercial dynamics. ATTR is a competitive market, AHP is a patient-finding market, OXLUMO is both. Do not apply competitive analysis frameworks to AHP or patient-finding frameworks to ATTR-CM. The analytical language must match the market reality.

  3. Be responsive to market events. Alnylam added ATTRUBY tracking rapidly after its November 2024 approval. Wainua fielding was paused strategically pending CARDIO-TTRansform data. The client expects ZoomRx to adapt study designs in real time as the competitive landscape evolves.

  4. Methodology precision matters. Alnylam actively discusses target list segmentation, survey length management, and recruitment match rates. This is a methodologically sophisticated client that will challenge sampling, trend comparability, and statistical significance. Come prepared.

  5. International insights should connect to the global narrative. The international PET is not 5 separate country studies -- it is one integrated view of AMVUTTRA's global competitive position. Always synthesize across countries before diving into country-specific detail.

Competitive Threats to the Relationship

Threat 1: Specialty Research Vendors in Cardiology

As Alnylam expands into cardiology, they may seek research partners with established cardiology panels and cardiologist relationships. If ZoomRx does not demonstrate strong cardiology recruitment capabilities and cardiology-specific analytical depth, a cardiology-native vendor could capture the ATTR-CM tracking budget.

Mitigation: Proactively demonstrate cardiologist-specific analysis in the PET (cardiologist SOV, message effectiveness by specialty, perception paradox insights). Show that ZoomRx's existing data already provides cardiology intelligence. Invest in cardiology panel quality.

Threat 2: International Research Vendors

The 5-country international PET requires local market recruitment and compliance expertise. Large international research companies (IQVIA, Ipsos, Kantar) have broader multi-country infrastructure. If fielding challenges arise or Alnylam's international team grows more autonomous, they may seek international-only vendors.

Mitigation: The cross-country synthesis is ZoomRx's moat. No international-only vendor can connect international PET data to US PET + ATU + PCA in the ADOPT framework. Ensure every international deliverable explicitly connects back to the US data.

Threat 3: Alnylam Internal Analytics

Alnylam is a sophisticated biotech with growing internal analytics capabilities. As the company scales toward $10B, they may build internal market research functions that replicate some tracking study capabilities.

Mitigation: Position ZoomRx's primary data as irreplaceable -- proprietary HCP panel, blinded competitive benchmarking, and cross-manufacturer tracking that internal teams cannot replicate. The 57K+ HCP proprietary panel and rare disease benchmarks are assets Alnylam cannot build internally.

Threat 4: Study Scope Compression from Target List Changes

The April 2026 target list change reduced available respondents by 56-72%. If recruitment becomes significantly harder, Alnylam may reduce study scope rather than increase incentives or fielding periods. This compresses ZoomRx revenue without reducing effort.

Mitigation: Proactively propose methodology bridges (old-TL to new-TL trend comparisons). Frame the smaller TL as an opportunity for higher-quality, more targeted data rather than a limitation. Demonstrate that the new segmentation (Ultra/High/Medium/Low/Exploratory) enables more precise insights.

Key Takeaway

The Alnylam relationship is deep, embedded, and growing -- but it is at a critical inflection point. The ATTR-CM approval is driving explosive growth that creates both opportunity (expanded research budgets) and risk (new competitors entering the vendor landscape as cardiology becomes the battleground). The principal's job is to ensure ZoomRx captures proportional value as Alnylam grows from a $3B to a $5.3B to a $10B company. The cross-study integration framework and international synthesis are the structural advantages that make ZoomRx difficult to displace -- but only if they are actively demonstrated, not assumed.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
